rationale: The citing paper uses Khan and Dhar (2006) in its core theoretical development to argue that prior factors can justify later self-indulgent or socially costly choices. It explicitly draws on this licensing logic when positioning its own mechanism, proposing price-based entitlement as another justification pathway for socially costly consumption. The citation is also revisited in the general discussion as part of the paper's contribution to the literature on justified less-virtuous behavior, indicating a meaningful framing role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Khan and Dhar (2006) as conceptual groundwork showing that justified licensing can shift consumers toward indulgent or socially costly options. It builds its framing on that precedent to introduce price entitlement as a distinct antecedent of similar justified consumption outcomes.
